description: Authenticated version-ancestry and exact-tag derivation vectors for SemVer-Trust §7.5 and §10 (ADR-029). Graphs, refs, authorities, and decision inputs are immutable fixtures referenced by vectors.

vectors.description: Explicit null version predecessor starts a new line; a MINOR decision derives v0.1.0 from synthetic v0.0.0.
A demoted first MINOR release derives v0.1.0-t0.1; iteration is not supplied.
An omitted predecessor field cannot be inferred as null new-line genesis.
Full-history inception may independently continue an authenticated legacy v0.9.4 line.
Legacy v0.9.4 may precede a later included adoption boundary without changing adoption disclosure.
Boundary and version predecessor may coincide only because both bindings independently name the same commit.
Recreating or moving an annotated tag object fails even when it still peels to the legacy commit.
A stable-looking raw ref cannot peel to a different commit.
A non-null bootstrap predecessor must resolve exactly; absence is not implicit genesis.
Genesis continuation requires one canonical clean §7.1 tag.
A descriptor with multiple candidate version predecessors fails instead of choosing the highest tag.
A component-qualified legacy tag cannot be replayed into a chain with another canonical prefix.
An adoption version predecessor must be an ancestor of or equal to the included boundary.
Identical authenticated adoption state cannot be redirected to v0.0.0 by a caller current-version input.
A caller cannot choose t0.7 for a first prerelease; the derived iteration is one.
The accepted predecessor v1.2.3, not a caller flag, supplies the recurring MINOR baseline.
A second T0 cut of unpromoted target v1.3.0 derives t0.2 from accepted state.
A re-cut of the first unpromoted target retains synthetic v0.0.0 and complete inception history as its baseline.
The first T2 cut of target v1.3.0 derives t2.1 even though T0 already has an iteration.
Legacy CLI values may assert the authenticated predecessor and derived iteration exactly, but do not select either.
An explicit advance abandons unpromoted v1.3.0 and derives PATCH target v1.3.1 from authenticated state.
Advancing the core does not let one T3 interval erase an unpromoted T0 predecessor lineage.
A pending MAJOR correction overrides an incremental PATCH decision and advances v1.3.0 to v2.0.0.
The pending MAJOR floor is applied before channel selection, so T3/high does not inherit PATCH-only differ demotion.
After advancing from a trust prerelease, the accepted trust tag and source remain the new target's compatibility baseline.
A MAJOR semantic floor cannot be hidden inside a re-cut of a MINOR target.
A clean T3 fix interval cannot erase the accepted T0 contribution already accumulated under the same target.
A caller assertion cannot upgrade the complete target lineage from T0 to T3.
An accepted semantic invalidation requires a corrective advance; another same-target re-cut is not allowed.
Two canonical tags in one accepted predecessor cannot silently select version state.
Version state is component-local even when another component has the same tag spelling.
A predecessor cannot bind MINOR as the bump from v1.2.2 while claiming target v1.2.3.
A pending PATCH correction is invalid when the bound target already used a MINOR bump.
Derived t0.2 cannot overwrite an existing tag, even if that tag points at an older accepted commit.
Promotion at the same TO emits clean v1.3.0 without applying another MINOR bump.
After a source successor is accepted, late promotion is attestation-only and cannot occupy the old target's clean tag.
Demotion cannot replace accepted clean v1.3.0 with a mutable tag; only the superseding attestation changes.
A newly proven MAJOR floor cannot rename an immutable MINOR target; the correction is attestation-only.
A same-head reevaluation that again proves the bound MINOR bump sufficient clears the pending correction and may promote.
